metric_value: 70% of heavy metals in US garbage dumps come from electronics.

metric_value: Aberdeen expects legally acceptable level of e-waste tolerance will continue to tighten; companies may be held retroactively accountable.
notes: Prediction.

metric_value: E-waste regulation tightened significantly around 2002-2003. The EU WEEE Directive (2002/96/EC) was issued in 2003 and required implementation by August 2004-2005. California enacted the Electronic Waste Recycling Act of 2003 (SB 20). This represented a major tightening of e-waste regulation, validating Aberdeen's prediction.
notes: Placeholder for Phase 3 verification. | E-waste regulations tightened in the 2002-2003 timeframe as predicted. EU WEEE Directive and California SB 20 both enacted. Source: https://calrecycle.ca.gov/electronics/statutes/; https://www.wilmerhale.com/en/insights/publications/new-eu-legislation-affects-electronic-waste-and-the-use-of-hazardous-materials-in-electric-and-electronic-devices-august-11-2003

metric_value: 77% of IT executives purchase secondary-market equipment; 46% plan to increase spending in that area by at least 15%.
notes: CIO Magazine, October 2002.

metric_value: Right time to dispose of a PC is ~36 months rather than 48 months.
notes: Aberdeen + Intel + Siemens Business Services joint research.

metric_value: PC disposal/replacement window did not shorten dramatically to less than 3 years for corporate desktops by 2003. Enterprise PC refresh cycles remained at approximately 3-5 years through the 2000s. The prediction of shorter replacement windows was not broadly validated; corporate lifecycles stabilized around 4 years.
notes: Placeholder for Phase 3 verification. | Corporate PC refresh cycles did not shift toward shorter windows; 3-5 year cycles persisted through the 2000s and beyond. Source: https://www.reddit.com/r/sysadmin/comments/4o2lgm/why_do_most_companies_refresh_their_machines_every_3_years/

Covers desktops, notebooks, servers, storage, networking, monitors, printers, projectors, keyboards, mice from any manufacturer.
Settlement Report (per-asset resale/recycle disposition) + Certificate of Disposal (overwrite + shred verification, EPA-compliant).
Aberdeen recommends enterprises not leave IT asset disposition to chance or generalists; partner with an end-of-life specialist.
Asset disposal costs deserve to be enumerated upfront in total cost of ownership; plan at point of purchase.
